source_pr.title: expression: Fix greatest and least function lost decimal precision compared with MySQL
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #25765 

Problem Summary:
Greatest and least function lost decimal precision compared with MySQL
see:
`select greatest(1.0, 2)` return `2` while MySQL returns `2.0`

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:

How it Works:

### Related changes

- PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test


Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more CPU
    - Consumes more MEM
- Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- `No release note`

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select id, greatest(nu, 1, 2), least(nu, 1.000, 3.0000) from t order by id").Check( (expression/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select id, greatest(nu, 1, 2), least(nu, 1.000, 3.0000) from t order by id").Check( (expression/integration_test.go)
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select id, greatest(nu, 1, 2), least(nu, 1.000, 3.0000) from t order by id").Check( (expression/integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select id, greatest(nu, 1, 2), least(nu, 1.000, 3.0000) from t order by id").Check( (expression/integration_test.go)
files: expression/builtin_compare.go, expression/builtin_compare_test.go, expression/builtin_compare_vec.go, expression/integration_test.go, types/mydecimal.go
